channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Democrats elect Ken Martin, the party leader in Minnesota, as their national chair
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — Democrats on Saturday elected Ken Martin, the party leader in Minnesota, as the national chair, turning to a low-profile Midwestern political operative to coordinate their resistance to Donald Trump’s presidency.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump puts tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China, spurring trade war as North American allies respond
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Saturday signed an order to impose stiff tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada and China, drawing swift retaliation and an undeniable sense of betrayal from the country's North American neighbors as a trade war erupted among the longtime allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ZEKE MILLER, ROB GILLIES and CHRISTOPHER SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Long-lost van Gogh painting was sold at Minnesota garage sale for $50, according to report
item.description: Experts believe a long-lost Vincent van Gogh painting, "Elimar," was found at a Minnesota garage sale in 2016, where it was sold for $50.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Data from the deadliest U.S. air accident in a generation show conflicting altitude readings
item.description: Recovery crews and divers searched the Potomac River for remains and cleared wreckage Saturday from the midair collision of a passenger jet and Army helicopter that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump signs tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China
item.description: "Tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China are SIGNED!" White House spokesperson Harrison Fields posted on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Officials recover 42 remains from Potomac River as investigation into midair collision continues
item.description: Officials said on Saturday that they recovered 42 sets of remains amid the investigation into the Jan. 29 Potomac River midair collision, and plan on releasing more details in coming days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Data from the deadliest U.S. air accident in a generation show conflicting altitude readings
item.description: Recovery crews and divers searched the Potomac River for remains and cleared wreckage Saturday from the midair collision of a passenger jet and Army helicopter that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Seven dead, 19 injured in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — A person in a car was the seventh fatal victim of the fiery crash of an air ambulance onto a busy Philadelphia street, authorities said Saturday, as investigators sifted through burned cars, damaged homes and charred debris for clues to determine why the aircraft plummeted shortl
item.dc:creator: MATT ROURKE and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USAID website goes offline in Trump administration's 2-week-old freeze on foreign aid worldwide
item.description: USAID’s website has dropped offline without explanation in the Trump administration's two-week-old funding freeze of U.S. foreign aid and development funding.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Third soldier inside Black Hawk helicopter involved in DC midair collision identified
item.description: The third soldier aboard a Black Hawk helicopter on a training mission when it collided with an American Airlines jet over Washington, D.C., Wednesday has been identified.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Army releases name of 3rd soldier who died in helicopter and airliner crash
item.description: The Army has released the name of the third soldier who died Wednesday when an Army helicopter collided with an American Airlines jet near the D.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to settle with woman who says ex-Officer Chauvin knelt on her back
item.description: The city of Minneapolis has agreed to pay $600,000 to settle a lawsuit by a woman who alleged that ex-Officer Derek Chauvin hauled her from her minivan and pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just as he did four months later when he killed George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Steve Karnowski, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: With plans in the works and troops on the way, military assumes a mostly familiar role on the border
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Dozens of Marines unfurled coils of concertina wire — one on the ground and one slightly above — making it more difficult to climb a border wall separating Tijuana from San Diego.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Why Trump's meme coin is a cash grab
item.description: The coin – officially called $Trump – briefly ascended into the top 15 cryptocurrencies by market capitalization and attracted over a half-million buyers.
item.dc:creator: Maximilian Brichta Doctoral Student of Communication, University of Southern California, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina executes man convicted of murder in state’s third execution since September
item.description: A South Carolina death row inmate was executed on Friday, marking the third time in four months the state has carried out the death penalty.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 10 Most Wanted List for killing his bride in Illinois captured in Mexico
item.description: A fugitive on the FBI's 10 Most Wanted list for allegedly killing his wife on their wedding night more than 12 years ago was captured in Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Seven dead, 19 injured in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia
item.description: At least seven people were killed and 19 were injured when an air ambulance crashed to the ground on a busy Philadelphia street, creating what witnesses described as a massive fireball and a chaotic street scene.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Matt Rourke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A deadly flight out of Wichita has one of America's most historic aviation cities reeling
item.description: WICHITA, Kan. (AP) — When American Eagle Flight 5342 took off from Wichita, the Midwest city with a proud aviation history was glowing from a big moment. It had just hosted the next generation of Olympic hopefuls at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A medical plane carrying a child patient and 5 others crashes in Philadelphia, setting homes ablaze
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — A medical transport jet with a child patient, her mother and four others aboard crashed into a Philadelphia neighborhood shortly after takeoff Friday evening, exploding in a fireball that engulfed several homes.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MATT ROURKE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: CBS agrees to hand over '60 Minutes' Harris interview transcripts to FCC
item.description: CBS says it will turn over unedited transcripts of its October interview with Kamala Harris to the Federal Communications Commission.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What is known about the fatal crash of a plane and Army chopper
item.description: American Airlines Flight 5342 hit an Army helicopter near Washington D.C.'s Reagan National Wednesday night, sending the two aircraft into the Potomac River and killing all 67 aboard in the deadliest U.S. air crash in more than two decades. The crash around 9 p.m.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Numerous goods could get more expensive due to Trump tariffs, experts say
item.description: The 25% tax that President Donald Trump plans to slap on imports from Canada and Mexico as soon as Saturday could drive up the price of everything from gasoline and pickup trucks, to Super Bowl party guacamole dip.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN AP Economics Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man rams cars while 'upset' over Nashville traffic: Police
item.description: Police say he was "apparently upset" about the congested downtown traffic after the Nashville Predators game.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Guerry, NewsNation

item.title: Wichita airport sets up memorial for DC plane crash victims
item.description: Wichita Dwight D. Eisenhower Airport set up a memorial for the 64 victims lost on an American Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Zena Taher, NewsNation

item.title: 40+ bodies pulled from Potomac near Reagan National
item.description: More than 40 bodies have been recovered from the Potomac River after a deadly mid-air collision sent an Army Black Hawk helicopter and American Airlines plane diving into the icy water earlier this week, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Trump says helicopter in crash was flying too high
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday asserted the military helicopter involved in a deadly midair collision near Washington, D.C., was "flying too high.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Congress authorized more flights at Reagan National despite warnings
item.description: Congress added more flights to Reagan National's schedule last year despite warnings from the airport.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump warns Canada, Mexico tariffs are coming on Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday said he plans to follow through on Saturday on his threat to impose 25 percent tariffs on goods from Mexico and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Civil rights attorney from Wichita, Kansas, victim of plane crash in DC
item.description: A woman who made a name for herself in Wichita and was in the process of doing the same thing in Washington was on the plane that crashed in Washington, D.C., on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Payton Steiner, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans advance Trump OMB nominee as Democrats boycott vote
item.description: Senate Republicans advanced the nomination of Russell Vought to lead the White House budget office as Democrats boycotted the meeting to protest the administration's recent efforts to freeze large swaths of federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard battles senators over Snowden, Syria in fiery hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, President Trump’s pick to be the next director of national intelligence, endured a difficult hearing before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Thursday as lawmakers pressed her on her views on Edward Snowden, spying, Syria and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: DC crash ‘almost identical’ to 1949 collision: Aviation expert
item.description: “There was a prior accident at Washington National 75 years ago almost identical to what happened last night.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, Fox 5

item.title: Midwest woman pardoned for Jan. 6 offenses sentenced in deadly drunken-driving crash
item.description: Emily Hernandez, a Missouri woman pardoned after her ties to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol, was sentenced Wednesday in connection with a drunken-driving crash.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Satellite imagery shows recovery efforts following deadly DC plane crash
item.description: Satellite imagery captured recovery efforts underway on the Potomac River Thursday following a plane crash that is being considered one of the deadliest in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump makes moves to expand power, sparking chaos, possible constitutional crisis
item.description: Just a little over a week into his second term, President Donald Trump is taking steps to maximize his power, sparking chaos and what critics contend is a constitutional crisis as he challenges the separation of powers that have defined American government for more than 200 years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
